result:
Here's a summary of the changes I made to fix the issue:

## Root Cause

CSV files weren't visible in AnkiDroid's file picker because the MIME type `text/csv` (the standard IANA MIME type for CSV files) was missing from key routing logic. On many Android devices, the system reports `.csv` files with the MIME type `text/csv`, but AnkiDroid only recognized `text/comma-separated-values` and `text/tab-separated-values` in its intent handling. Additionally, some devices (especially those with Excel installed) report CSV files as `application/vnd.ms-excel`, which wasn't in the file picker's allowed MIME types.

## Changes Made

### 1. `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/dialogs/ImportFileSelectionFragment.kt` (line 106)
Added `application/vnd.ms-excel` to the `extraMimes` array for the CSV file picker. Some Android devices report `.csv` files with this MIME type (particularly when Microsoft Excel is installed), so the file picker needs to accept it to show those files.

### 2. `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/IntentHandler.kt` (line 338)
Added `text/csv` to the MIME type check in `getLaunchType()`. Previously, only `text/tab-separated-values` and `text/comma-separated-values` were checked, causing files with the standard `text/csv` MIME type to fall through to the `FILE_IMPORT` handler (which treats them as APKG/COLPKG files and fails).

### 3. `AnkiDroid/src/main/AndroidManifest.xml` (line 244)
Added `<data android:mimeType="text/csv"/>` to the MIME type intent-filter. This allows Android to route files with `text/csv` MIME type to AnkiDroid when opened from external file managers.
